text: Heavy resistance training as an adjunct to running training improves running economy with a small effect size, while plyometric training shows only a trivial effect on running economy.
Heavy resistance training improves running time trial performance more than plyometric training when added to running training.
Heavy resistance training with nearly maximal loads (≥90% 1RM) provides greater improvements in running economy than training with lower loads (<90% 1RM).
Longer heavy resistance training periods (10-14 weeks) produce greater running economy improvements than shorter periods (6-8 weeks).
Longer plyometric training periods (8-10 weeks) produce greater running economy improvements than shorter periods (4-6 weeks).
For distance runners seeking to improve running economy through strength training, heavy resistance training with loads at or above 90% of 1RM is the most effective approach.
